# synology-apm infra — Site, Servers, Storage, Hypervisors

Read-only commands for APM infrastructure. `synology-apm infra server list --verbose` is the
primary source for backup server **namespaces**, used as `--namespace` in many other
commands.

See [apm-shared](../apm-shared/SKILL.md) for global options, output formats, and
confirmation rules.

## Commands

### `synology-apm infra info`

Show APM site information, Management Server details, and site storage statistics.

```
Usage: synology-apm infra info [OPTIONS]
```

### `synology-apm infra server list`

List all backup servers in the cluster.

```
Usage: synology-apm infra server list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--search <SEARCH>` | Keyword search |
| `--status <healthy/warning/critical/disconnected/syncing, ...>` | Filter by status; repeatable |
| `--type <dp/nas, ...>` | Filter by server type; repeatable |

### `synology-apm infra server get`

Show details for a backup server.

**Search mode (name keyword):**

```sh
synology-apm infra server get "apm-server-01"
```

**Direct mode:**

```sh
synology-apm infra server get --id <server-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm infra server get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Server name keyword (search mode) |
| `--id <SERVER_ID>` | Backup Server ID (direct mode; from synology-apm infra server list --verbose) |

### `synology-apm infra server change-plan`

Apply or remove a Tiering Plan on a backup server.

**Apply a tiering plan (search mode):**

```sh
synology-apm infra server change-plan "apm-server-01" --plan "30-Day Tiering"
```

**Apply a tiering plan (direct mode):**

```sh
synology-apm infra server change-plan --id <server-id> --plan <plan-id>
```

**Remove the current tiering plan:**

```sh
synology-apm infra server change-plan "apm-server-01" --remove
```

```
Usage: synology-apm infra server change-plan [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Server name keyword (search mode) |
| `--id <SERVER_ID>` | Backup Server ID (direct mode; from synology-apm infra server list --verbose) |
| `--plan <PLAN>` | Tiering plan name or ID to apply |
| `--remove` | Remove the current tiering plan |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip confirmation prompt |

> This action prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

### `synology-apm infra storage list`

List all remote storage devices.

```
Usage: synology-apm infra storage list [OPTIONS]
```

### `synology-apm infra storage get`

Show details for a remote storage device.

**Search mode (name or endpoint):**

```sh
synology-apm infra storage get "DSM-Storage"
```

**Direct mode:**

```sh
synology-apm infra storage get --id <storage-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm infra storage get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Remote Storage display name or endpoint (search mode) |
| `--id <STORAGE_ID>` | Remote Storage UUID (direct mode; from synology-apm infra storage list --verbose) |

### `synology-apm infra hypervisor list`

List all registered hypervisor inventory servers.

```
Usage: synology-apm infra hypervisor list [OPTIONS]
```

### `synology-apm infra hypervisor get`

Show details for a hypervisor inventory server.

**Search mode (hostname or address):**

```sh
synology-apm infra hypervisor get "esxi1.example.com"
```

**Direct mode:**

```sh
synology-apm infra hypervisor get --id <hypervisor-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm infra hypervisor get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Hypervisor hostname or address (search mode) |
| `--id <HYPERVISOR_ID>` | Hypervisor UUID (direct mode; from synology-apm infra hypervisor list --verbose) |

## Tips

- `infra server list`'s `role` field: `LEADER` = Primary Management Server,
  `REPLICA` = Secondary Management Server; other values are regular backup servers.
- Use `--verbose`/`-v` on `server list` for Description/Server ID/Namespace, on
  `storage list` for Remote Storage ID, and on `hypervisor list` for Hypervisor ID.
